data.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification): all codebase assertions in the Cluster 1 plan were Read/Grep-verified against source and hold.

Verified PASSES:
- `canonical_str(event)` is positional-only today with no `ascii_only`/`default` (canonical.py:39-47) — additive-param premise holds.
- The six "untouched" importers all call positionally: event_append.py:114, _commands/txn.py:220/394/415, delete.py:114/205, compact.py:139, inbound_translate.py:239, batch_dispatch.py:238.
- signing.py:198 `_canonical_payload` uses `ensure_ascii=False` → maps to seam default `ascii_only=False` (byte-identical).
- schema.py:396 `canonical_json` uses `ensure_ascii=False`; `content_hash` = sha256(canonical_json) (schema.py:399-403).
- conflict_resolver.py:217-219 `_hash_value` = `json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True, default=str, separators=(",",":"))` with NO `ensure_ascii` — confirms the load-bearing `ascii_only=True` (stdlib default True) and `default=str` claims.
- mutation.py:106 `serialize_manifest` uses explicit `ensure_ascii=True` → `ascii_only=True` preserve claim holds.
- llm/workflow/__init__.py:85-86 exports `canonical_json`/`content_hash` in `__all__` — re-export-preservation premise holds.
- Seam import path resolves from inside rebar_reconciler (existing `from rebar._store.canonical import canonical_str`).
- A compact-separator grep finds only the four migration targets + the seam; no missed duplicate sorted-key encoder.
- canonical.py is 47 LOC, well under the 800-LOC cap.

No E4 findings.
